message:
chore: beads flush + refresh ext4_inode_inline_data_with_continuation checksum

Two small housekeeping updates:

- .beads/issues.jsonl: another single-row br flush export refreshing an
  already-tracked bead's metadata; no new IDs introduced. Keeps the
  committed JSONL aligned with the live SQLite state.

- conformance/fixtures/checksums.sha256: refresh the SHA-256 pin for
  ext4_inode_inline_data_with_continuation.json. The fixture JSON
  itself was regenerated in a prior commit by a sibling agent (the
  inline-data continuation block's expected layout was re-serialised);
  the checksum pin line is updated in lockstep so conformance runs
  that verify fixture integrity stop failing on the stale hash. All
  other entries in the manifest remain at their previous pins.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"bd-j3q8m","title":"[HIGH] rch workspace cargo test can lose the built ffs-btrfs test binary before execution","description":"# Review finding

A fresh remote workspace gate failed with infrastructure-style `ENOENT` before running the `ffs-btrfs` unit test binary, even though the build had already progressed through most of the workspace.

## Evidence
- Command: `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets`
- Worker/job: `vmi1156319`, job `j-29801994268977526`
- Failure tail:
  - `Running unittests src/lib.rs (/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod/debug/deps/ffs_btrfs-73268ec8690d5672)`
  - `could not execute process ... ffs_btrfs-73268ec8690d5672 (never executed)`
  - `No such file or directory (os error 2)`
- Total runtime before failure: ~468s, after benches and most crates had already run successfully.

## Why this is high severity
This makes the review gate itself nondeterministic. It can mask real product failures behind remote artifact-loss / target-dir-race behavior, which blocks reliable triage and any attempt to prove workspace green.

## Expected fix direction
Investigate remote worker target-dir lifecycle for long `cargo test --workspace --all-targets` runs: artifact eviction, concurrent reuse of `/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod`, or `rch` post-build handling that removes test binaries before execution. Add a deterministic repro or worker-side guard so the built test binary remains executable through the full run.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:18:09Z","created_by":"cod-fs","updated_at":"2026-04-22T21:16:55.243435546Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["qa","rch","review","workspace-gate"]}
+{"id":"bd-j3q8m","title":"[HIGH] rch workspace cargo test can lose the built ffs-btrfs test binary before execution","description":"# Review finding

A fresh remote workspace gate failed with infrastructure-style `ENOENT` before running the `ffs-btrfs` unit test binary, even though the build had already progressed through most of the workspace.

## Evidence
- Command: `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets`
- Worker/job: `vmi1156319`, job `j-29801994268977526`
- Failure tail:
  - `Running unittests src/lib.rs (/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod/debug/deps/ffs_btrfs-73268ec8690d5672)`
  - `could not execute process ... ffs_btrfs-73268ec8690d5672 (never executed)`
  - `No such file or directory (os error 2)`
- Total runtime before failure: ~468s, after benches and most crates had already run successfully.

## Why this is high severity
This makes the review gate itself nondeterministic. It can mask real product failures behind remote artifact-loss / target-dir-race behavior, which blocks reliable triage and any attempt to prove workspace green.

## Expected fix direction
Investigate remote worker target-dir lifecycle for long `cargo test --workspace --all-targets` runs: artifact eviction, concurrent reuse of `/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod`, or `rch` post-build handling that removes test binaries before execution. Add a deterministic repro or worker-side guard so the built test binary remains executable through the full run.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:18:09Z","created_by":"cod-fs","updated_at":"2026-04-22T21:16:55.243435546Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["qa","rch","review","workspace-gate"]}
 {"id":"bd-j4256","title":"add mounted-path btrfs read-only EROFS coverage for create mkdir link and symlink","description":"Survey finding: btrfs fuse_e2e proves create, mkdir, hard-link, and symlink success on writable mounts, but the mounted-path read-only refusal contract for those mutations is not frozen even though ffs-fuse rejects them with EROFS. Scope: add read-only btrfs probes that attempt create, mkdir, hard_link, and symlink and assert exact EROFS with unchanged directory contents. Acceptance: btrfs FUSE E2E freezes read-only operator-visible refusal for these entry-creation mutations.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:26:16.636621634Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T19:36:45.456797742Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T19:36:45.456797742Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-j960","title":"Align README architecture/dependency claims with source manifests","description":"Source-level investigation found README drift against Cargo manifests: (1) README claims asupersync/ftui must be sibling checkouts, but workspace uses crates.io versions; (2) architecture section implies direct ffs-core -> ffs-repair write-path dependency, while manifests route repair through ffs-mvcc; (3) harness placement/dependency wording can be tightened to match actual crate graph. Update README to match current codebase architecture and dependency reality.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-02-25T23:40:32.586340068Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T23:41:15.301461460Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T23:41:15.301443086Z","close_reason":"README now matches source manifests for dependency sourcing and repair/layering wording","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","docs","readme"]}

 {"id":"bd-r7od","title":"Add generic/112 preallocation conformance coverage for ext4 and btrfs","description":"Upstream xfstests generic/112 runs fsx with -A and -x to exercise AIO plus preallocated unwritten extents. FrankenFS already has fallocate tests, but the harness lacks an explicit cross-filesystem conformance check for the preallocation contract that generic/112 depends on. Add deterministic ext4+btrfs harness coverage proving that preallocation extends size, unwritten regions read as zeroes, writes into preallocated space preserve surrounding zero-fill, and the contract survives reopen.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T17:23:32.225284126Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T18:00:09.368172019Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T18:00:09.365241100Z","close_reason":"Added generic/112 preallocation conformance coverage for ext4 and btrfs via shared harness assertions","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-r7vx0","title":"[HIGH] restore missing fuzz_ioctl_dispatch corpus dir for crash promotion pipeline","description":"Workspace gate regression from `cargo test --workspace --all-targets` on 2026-04-22 via `rch` worker `vmi1156319`:

- `crash_promotion::tests::corpus_directories_exist` panics because `fuzz/corpus/fuzz_ioctl_dispatch` is missing
- `crash_promotion::tests::pipeline_validation_passes` then fails with `22/23 corpus directories found`

Root cause is repo drift between the fuzz target inventory and the shipped corpus directories. Acceptance:
1. `fuzz/corpus/fuzz_ioctl_dispatch/` exists with at least one seed or the pipeline no longer requires it.
2. `crates/ffs-harness/src/crash_promotion.rs` inventory and repo layout are consistent.
3. `cargo test -p ffs-harness crash_promotion -- --nocapture` passes via `rch`.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:45:08.560673853Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:45:08.560673853Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-r8c8r","title":"[HIGH] fixture checksum manifest drifted for ext4 inline-data continuation JSON","description":"Reproduced from `env AGENT_NAME=cod-fs CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets` on 2026-04-22.

Failure:
- `fixture_checksum_manifest_is_complete` at `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs:3680`
- fixture: `ext4_inode_inline_data_with_continuation.json`
- manifest digest: `b040217daafc0d5a7706a9263652b33776989f5b7c5c7f76e807a243860f6218`
- actual digest: `5f811167369ea9e1bee12196a8ce72f070cd08105d85473401e22dc28a646525`

The fixture content and checksum manifest are out of sync, so the conformance gate stays red even when parser behavior is otherwise stable.

Acceptance:
- fixture and checksum manifest agree again
- `fixture_checksum_manifest_is_complete` passes
- `full_conformance_gate_pass` is no longer blocked by this manifest slice.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:25:18.016914474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:25:18.016914474Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","ext4","fixtures","review"]}
+{"id":"bd-r8c8r","title":"[HIGH] fixture checksum manifest drifted for ext4 inline-data continuation JSON","description":"Reproduced from `env AGENT_NAME=cod-fs CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets` on 2026-04-22.

Failure:
- `fixture_checksum_manifest_is_complete` at `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs:3680`
- fixture: `ext4_inode_inline_data_with_continuation.json`
- manifest digest: `b040217daafc0d5a7706a9263652b33776989f5b7c5c7f76e807a243860f6218`
- actual digest: `5f811167369ea9e1bee12196a8ce72f070cd08105d85473401e22dc28a646525`

The fixture content and checksum manifest are out of sync, so the conformance gate stays red even when parser behavior is otherwise stable.

Acceptance:
- fixture and checksum manifest agree again
- `fixture_checksum_manifest_is_complete` passes
- `full_conformance_gate_pass` is no longer blocked by this manifest slice.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:25:18.016914474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T21:19:07.491927192Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","ext4","fixtures","review"]}
 {"id":"bd-r9n2u","title":"add ffs-core fuzz target for path-component validation","description":"Apply /testing-fuzzing to crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs::validate_single_path_component(name: &[u8]). Scope: add a very high-throughput libFuzzer target over raw path bytes, seeded with valid ASCII names plus boundary cases for empty, dot, dot-dot, slash-containing, NUL-containing, overlong, and non-UTF8 names. Assert totality (no panics), deterministic errno-class mapping, and compatibility with the callers that use this gate for create, link, symlink, rename, and xattr-adjacent path mutations. Acceptance: new fuzz target registered in fuzz/Cargo.toml, corpus seeds added, and any minimized crashers promoted to regression tests.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T05:59:00Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:06:57Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:06:57Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ffs-core","fuzz","paths","testing","validation"]}
 {"id":"bd-raks","title":"Fix missing FsGeometry fields in ffs-inode tests","description":"## Purpose

Restore workspace compilation by updating stale `FsGeometry` initializers in `ffs-inode` to include the required `desc_size` and `feature_ro_compat` fields.

## Scope

1. Reproduce the current `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` failure via `rch`.
2. Patch the failing `FsGeometry` initializers in `crates/ffs-inode/src/lib.rs`.
3. Re-run targeted `rch` checks for `ffs-inode` and, if clean, the workspace check.

## Acceptance criteria
- `crates/ffs-inode/src/lib.rs` compiles with current `FsGeometry` definition
- Targeted `rch exec -- cargo check -p ffs-inode --all-targets` passes
- Workspace check moves past this failure or reports the next unrelated blocker
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-20T03:40:47.448066803Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-20T20:09:05.994009750Z","closed_at":"2026-03-20T20:09:05.993264249Z","close_reason":"Patched missing FsGeometry fields in ffs-inode tests; targeted rch cargo check -p ffs-inode --all-targets passed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","inode","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-rale","title":"Improve FfsError::Io errno mapping with ErrorKind fallback","description":"When std::io::Error has no raw_os_error() (e.g. created via Error::new()), to_errno() falls back to EIO. Improve by mapping io::ErrorKind to appropriate errnos (NotFound→ENOENT, PermissionDenied→EACCES, etc.) before falling back to EIO. This gives FUSE callers more specific error codes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-26T00:49:02.204994325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T00:59:08.073573327Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T00:59:08.073546988Z","close_reason":"Improved to_errno() Io variant: now maps ErrorKind to specific errnos (NotFound→ENOENT, PermissionDenied→EACCES, AlreadyExists→EEXIST, WouldBlock→EAGAIN, InvalidInput/InvalidData→EINVAL, TimedOut→ETIMEDOUT, Interrupted→EINTR) when raw_os_error() is None, instead of always falling back to EIO. Added test io_error_kind_mapping_without_raw_errno covering all mapped kinds. Also fixed two pre-existing clippy pedantic lints. All 20 tests pass, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
